behavior_in_task_description.reason: All asserted paths (wkBrowser.ts, browsers.json, deviceDescriptorsSource.json, README.md, release-notes-*.md, SKILL.md, webkit-safari-version.md) and required content (BROWSER_VERSION, wkBrowser reference, Safari/stable mention) are named in instruction.md; the version 26.4 is discoverable via the cited Safari Release Notes URL.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes what to update but doesn't provide the exact version string '26.4' — agent must look it up from Safari release notes.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests check version strings and content substrings, not specific phrasing or implementation details; skill doc checks are for required keywords only.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests parse JSON, extract regex from TS source, and execute subprocess checks — not just substring matching, though the domain is inherently text-based config.

test_not_tautological.reason: Tests assert version equals '26.4' and check content across multiple files; a stub cannot pass without making actual edits.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All behaviors in instruction.md (version updates across 5 file groups, skill doc creation, SKILL.md link) are tested.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests grouped with section comments (fail_to_pass, pass_to_pass static, repo_tests) and descriptive docstrings.

structured_data_schema.reason: browsers.json schema (browsers array, name, browserVersion, revision keys) and device descriptor structure are adequately implied by the files-to-look-at list.

no_typos.reason: All filenames and paths appear consistent between instruction, tests, and solution.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction names files to modify, but localization isn't the puzzle — the task is finding the right version; this is appropriate scope.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: Gold patch uses targeted sed replacements on specific version strings, not full-file rewrites.
